quotes.text: complexity very, very bad. say again: complexity very bad. you not smart enough to manage it, nobody is.
quotes.author: The Grug Brained Developer

quotes.text: grug very impressed with type systems when young grug. type systems shiny and wonderful. then grug mass mass if/else on union type and mass mass casting everywhere and grug not mass impressed anymore.
quotes.author: The Grug Brained Developer

quotes.text: grug wonder why big brain take simple monolith, split into 200 microservices, then mass mass effort to make work like monolith again.
quotes.author: The Grug Brained Developer

quotes.text: grug have cardinal rule: never refactor and change functionality at same time. grug see mass mass senior devs do this and mass mass problems every time.
quotes.author: The Grug Brained Developer

quotes.text: grug say always, always, always implement thing completely wrong way first, then fix. abstraction is the enemy of understanding for grug.
quotes.author: The Grug Brained Developer

quotes.text: grug no mass mass like closure. closure make code look mass smart but grug mass mass debug closure and mass mass not fun.
quotes.author: The Grug Brained Developer

quotes.text: The competent programmer is fully aware of the strictly limited size of his own skull; therefore he approaches the programming task in full humility.
quotes.author: Edsger W. Dijkstra
quotes.source: The Humble Programmer, 1972 Turing Award Lecture

quotes.text: When in doubt, use brute force.
quotes.author: Ken Thompson

quotes.text: Trailers are supposed to be pretty dumb. This is what makes them valuable from a logistical perspective. If we hyper focus on reducing the carbon footprint of each specific shipment we may find we created an overall increase in aggregate once the higher order and deferred consequences come to bear.
quotes.author: bob1029
quotes.source: Hacker News — Self-Powered Trailers Promise Leaner Freight Runs
quotes.extraction_note: Pithy insight on simplicity vs. over-optimization in systems design, directly applicable to software engineering.

quotes.text: Maybe I’m taking crazy pills, but I’m still stuck on “why the hell does a TUI need to run in terminal React by way of JavaScript” The fact that Anthropic felt the need to buy a runtime so they could make their TUI better speaks more to the quality of engineering than anything else IMO. If rewrites are so easy, why not rewrite CC in a native language? Would’ve been a hell of a lot cheaper.
quotes.author: weakfish
quotes.source: Hacker News — Claude Code uses Bun written in Rust now
quotes.extraction_note: Humorous critique of unnecessary complexity in software toolchains, with a grug-brained call for simplicity.

quotes.text: GPT didn't make any progress reverse engineering ancient Corel Draw documents until I told it to ignore the open source libraries. LibreOffice's import code was half-baked, and silently changing Arial to Liberation Sans wasn't particularly liberating.
quotes.author: badlibrarian
quotes.source: Hacker News — How proprietary formats have become Microsoft's main tool for lock-in
quotes.extraction_note: Telling GPT to ignore OSS libraries to actually get work done is peak grug. The Liberation Sans quip is genuinely funny dev humor. Anti-bloat wisdom disguised as a war story.

quotes.text: I don't know what I'm doing. But my LLM does. I set up site-to-site VPNs with Mikrotek in fifteen minutes, rather than the day-long exercise of googling and praying.
quotes.author: zulux
quotes.source: Hacker News — HomeLab #1: MikroTik as a Home Router
quotes.extraction_note: Humorous, standalone insight about using LLMs to bypass complexity in networking, resonant with tech pragmatism and anti-complexity ethos.
